# UC-15.1.31 · APC InRow and Airflow Unit Compressor or Fan Fault Alarms

## Description

APC InRow and aisle containment products emit SNMP traps when compressors stall, fans fail, or airflow is inadequate. Landing those traps in Splunk gives the same early warning as UPS events for thermal runaway in edge and small data halls.

## Value

Prevents thermal shutdowns of IT loads by correlating cooling faults with rack inlet temperatures before CRAC capacity is exhausted.

## Implementation

Point NMC/trap agents to SC4SNMP trap listener or SNMP MI. Normalize `varbind` values into `alarm_text`. For polled architectures, create `snmp:apc:cooling` with extracted `coolingUnitState` / alarm fields per your MIB walk. Filter vendor maintenance windows. Tie alerts to on-call facilities, not only IT.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Trap sourcetype with `full_json` or `VARBINDS` extracted.
• HVAC vendor contract for onsite response.

Step 1 — Lab inject trap sample.

Step 2 — Deduplicate flapping traps with `streamstats`.

Step 3 — Map to BMS points if dual-monitored.

Step 4 — Thermal runbook links to rack shutdown SOP.

## SPL

```spl
index=power (sourcetype=snmp:trap OR sourcetype=snmp:apc:cooling) host=* trapoid="*318*"
| search cooling OR compressor OR Compressor OR fan OR Fan OR InRow OR Airflow OR airflow OR environmental OR Environmental
| table _time, host, trapoid, trap_name, eventName, _raw
```

## Visualization

Timeline (cooling traps), Table (host, alarm_text), Sankey (trap → rack group via lookup).

- [APC — InRow cooling overview](https://www.apc.com/us/en/product-range-solutions/row-cooling/)
